location.office_time_required: 4 days a week in Mountain View office

level_signals: 5+ years of experience in product management stated directly
Comp range $170K-$250K base consistent with Senior IC at a high-growth AI startup
Expected to shape product roadmap and product team itself — outsized scope for lean team

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 5+ years of experience in product management, with a track record of owning and shipping meaningful product areas in B2B SaaS, enterprise software, AI products, analytics products, or workflow/intelligence platforms.
You have a strong product sense for translating complex, ambiguous problems into clear product experiences, and you are comfortable moving between strategy and detailed execution.
You have a deep interest in AI and enterprise software, and you are excited by the opportunity to shape products that combine context, workflows, analytics, and proactive intelligence.

You have strong technical fluency and are comfortable partnering with engineering and data teams on systems involving structured and unstructured data, knowledge graphs, retrieval, or intelligence layers.

You can demonstrate granular product thinking, including how a user should experience a workflow, what the right interaction model is, and how to sequence product capabilities into something customers will actually adopt.
You are comfortable operating in highly ambiguous spaces and can create clarity, momentum, and alignment even when the product area is still emerging.
You are an excellent written and verbal communicator.
You have a proven track record of taking ownership, taking initiative, and delivering results.
You collaborate effectively with cross-functional partners.
You are an early adopter in building with or adopting AI for your own product craft and you are excited about helping customers accelerate their own AI adoption journey.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have a learning and growth mindset.
You are mission-first and understand that your success is measured by your product and team's success.

scope.team_size_estimate: lean product team (exact size not stated)
scope.surface: Enterprise Intelligence product area — proactive insights, organizational visibility, dashboards, recommendations, and decision-support tools for leaders and teams
primary_metrics_inferred: Customer value delivered via actionable insights
Measurable business impact for enterprise customers
core_responsibilities: Define and drive product strategy and roadmap for Enterprise Intelligence
Spend significant time with customers, prospects, and internal stakeholders to understand high-value enterprise intelligence use cases
Build products that turn Glean's enterprise context layer, graph, and connected system intelligence into actionable workflows, dashboards, recommendations, and proactive experiences
Partner with engineering, design, and data teams to turn ambiguous concepts into well-scoped products
Drive customer-focused prioritization and efficient execution
Collaborate with go-to-market, solutions, support, and forward-deployed teams
Help establish product foundations for a new category of Enterprise Intelligence
Bring a high bar for product craft including concrete workflows, UI behavior, and adoption paths

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as eligible for certain roles

green_flags: Clear product surface and ownership area for an emerging but well-framed category
Described as 'outsized role' in shaping both roadmap and product team — high impact opportunity
Well-known, award-winning company (Fast Company Top 10, Forbes AI 50, CNBC Disruptor 50)
Lean team structure signals high autonomy and visibility
red_flags: 4 days/week in Mountain View office is a very high in-office requirement that eliminates most remote candidates
Very wide comp range ($170K-$250K, $80K spread) makes it difficult to gauge level alignment without further information
Emerging product area with high ambiguity may result in shifting priorities and unclear success metrics early on
Visa sponsorship not addressed in JD
Buzzword density is high — 'superintelligent enterprise', 'enterprise context layer', 'proactive intelligence' may obscure concrete product scope

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years requirement + comp range)
